description_long: This house features a weathered, rustic exterior with faded, whitewashed wooden siding and a prominent, steeply pitched gable roof covered in aged, reddish-brown shingles. The roof displays visible wear, with several missing or damaged shingles and a small dormer window on one side. The front facade includes a recessed entryway with a small overhanging awning and a single step, flanked by two tall, narrow windows. On one end, a bay window projects outward, adding architectural interest and natural light to the interior. The windows are multi-paned and appear slightly distressed, matching the overall aged aesthetic. The structure is compact, single-story, and evokes the charm of early 20th-century rural or cottage-style homes.
description: A weathered, single-story house with faded wooden siding, a steep gable roof of aged shingles, and a bay window. The entryway features a small awning and tall, narrow windows, giving it a rustic, cottage-like appearance.
description_view_0: This view shows the long side of the house with two windows and the main entryway, topped by a small awning and a visibly worn roof.
description_view_1: This end view highlights the projecting bay window and the steep pitch of the roof, as well as a small dormer window above.
description_view_2: The opposite long side is visible here, featuring two windows and a clearer view of the roof's weathered shingles and missing sections.
description_view_3: This view focuses on the front entryway with its overhanging awning, flanked by two tall windows, and the steep gable roof above.
attribute_entryway_design: Recessed entry with a small overhanging awning, single step, and flanked by tall, narrow windows
